description_long: This is a green biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings supported by struts and wires, giving it a classic early 20th-century appearance. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the rear, with a rounded nose housing a wooden two-blade propeller and visible twin exhaust stacks on top. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, featuring a single brown seat. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both with simple, angular shapes. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted or omitted in this depiction. The overall design is reminiscent of vintage stunt or military aircraft, with a robust and functional aesthetic.
description: A green vintage biplane with dual wings, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller, featuring classic early aviation styling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, central propeller, and twin exhaust stacks above the nose.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the stacked wings, streamlined fuselage, and exposed propeller.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, open cockpit, and the symmetry of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the engine exhausts, cockpit seat, and the full length of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings mounted one above the other, supported by interplane struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with visible twin exhaust stacks.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; configuration indeterminate.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
